question: Will Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange exceed 8500 USD/tonne on May 31, 2026?
reason: Indonesia's significant nickel production cuts have led to a substantial market deficit, driving prices above $18,000 per tonne.
evidence: Indonesia's 2026 nickel mining quota reduced by 34% to 250–260 million tonnes, causing global supply concerns.
LME nickel prices surged to a 1½-year high of $18,950 per tonne on January 29, 2026.
Analysts predict a 32,000-tonne deficit in 2026, the first since 2021, due to reduced Indonesian output.
Nickel prices have stabilized between $17,000 and $17,400 per tonne as markets adjust to tighter quotas.
Indonesia's policy shift aims to regulate supply volatility and support prices.
The International Nickel Study Group forecasts a 32,000-tonne deficit in 2026, the first since 2021.
Analysts predict a 32,000-tonne deficit in 2026, the first since 2021, due to reduced Indonesian output.
